atoms.semantic_claim: Ethereum beacon consensus validates header extra_data with the ChainSpec-provided optional maximum instead of the upstream hard-coded default, preserving chain-specific extra_data acceptance/rejection behavior for both post-merge and pre-merge validation paths.

destinations.evidence: The port captures the ChainSpec max_extra_data_bytes value in EthBeaconConsensus and passes it into validate_header_extra_data during header validation.

destinations.snippet: let Some(max_size) = max_size else { return Ok(()) }; if extra_data_len > max_size { Err(ConsensusError::ExtraDataExceedsMax { len: extra_data_len }) } else { Ok(()) }
destinations.evidence: The called helper enforces Some(limit) and accepts None, matching the source consensus semantics.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Construct EthBeaconConsensus with ChainSpecBuilder::mainnet().max_extra_data_bytes(None) and validate a header with >32 bytes extra_data; it must pass, while the same header with Some(32) must fail with ExtraDataExceedsMax.
atoms.notes: Other hunks in this file are rustfmt/semicolon-only noise; the consensus-visible atom is exact in the port.

summary: Header extra_data validation now consumes ChainSpec max_extra_data_bytes, and the port carries the same consensus behavior.
notes: Destination evidence was spot-checked in ethereum/consensus and consensus/common.
